name: vercel
description: Vercel-inspired zot theme based on gantoreno/vscode-vercel. Uses the same core palette as the VS Code theme: pink keywords, blue constants/links, purple functions, green strings/success, amber warnings, and neutral Vercel grays for chrome.

color_descriptions.fg: Default foreground text, based on Vercel editor.foreground / terminal.foreground.
color_descriptions.muted: Secondary text, dividers, gutters, footers, inactive hints, based on Vercel foreground/sidebar/status gray.
color_descriptions.accent: Primary UI accent for prompt bars, bullets, headings, and active markers; mapped to Vercel keyword pink for more visible theme identity.
color_descriptions.user: User role label color; kept for compatibility with older UI surfaces.
color_descriptions.user_bubble_bg: Background behind user message rows, based on Vercel editor.selectionBackground / lineHighlightBackground.
color_descriptions.user_bubble_fg: Text foreground inside user message rows.
color_descriptions.assistant: Assistant/zot accent color and welcome label, mapped to Vercel blue.
color_descriptions.tool: Tool names, successful marks, additions in diffs, based on Vercel green.
color_descriptions.error: Errors, refused tool calls, deletions in diffs, based on Vercel error/list error colors.
color_descriptions.warning: Warnings and high context-usage state, based on Vercel modified/warning amber.
color_descriptions.spinner: Secondary animated/accent color, based on Vercel magenta/purple token color.
color_descriptions.selection_bg: Background for highlighted menu/list rows, based on Vercel selection background.
color_descriptions.syntax_base_style: Base Chroma style name used before syntax overrides.
color_descriptions.syntax: Token colors for markdown code fences, file previews, and diff code, mapped from VS Code tokenColors. Values are Chroma style entries.
